notes: Forward re-audit of 1881..18FC does not produce a trustworthy anchored owner: the region contains repeated opcode-looking slices, partial hardware-register write sequences, and a tempting helper-like tail ending in RTL, but none of those starts can be defended from validated caller context yet.

Raw same-bank target patterns inside the 1880s, including 188C, 1898, and 18A1, currently trace back to unresolved higher-bank C3 regions, so they are not strong enough to rescue those addresses into confirmed executable owners.

The next seam is reduced to 18FD because it is the next raw target candidate in-order after the frozen blob, but it should still be treated as unproven until its caller context is validated rather than merely pattern-matched.
This pass is intentionally conservative: it preserves one potentially meaningful candidate pocket around the 18FD area while refusing to overclaim the unstable middle after the 1880 RTL stub.
